display.purpose: Hero overall score only. Geist Mono so the number reads as an instrument readout.

headline.purpose: Page titles and score-panel section titles.

body.purpose: All prose: insight messages, form labels, helper text.

label.purpose: Column headers and small metadata tags, uppercase tracked.

data.purpose: Timestamps, slide references, session IDs — anything measured.

shadows.name: interactive-lift
shadows.purpose: The only shadow in the system. Hover/focus feedback on buttons only, never present at rest.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default ease-out-quint for all transitions and entrances (toasts, hover states).

motion.name: ease-ring-draw
motion.purpose: One-time stroke-draw entrance for the score ring, ~600ms, the system's single choreographed moment.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main CTA — Start Recording, Get Started, Start Session.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #6cc4ff; color: #0f1419; font-family: Geist, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; padding: 12px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), box-shadow 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #54b8fb; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6cc4ff; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background: #3aa3eb; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Non-primary actions — Copy share link, live-feedback toggle label.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #1a2028; color: #e8eef4; font-family: Geist, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem; padding: 10px 16px; border: 1px solid #2a323c; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #232b35; border-color: #3a4450; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.3); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #6cc4ff; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Score sub-category panel (Voice & Delivery, Body Language, etc).
components.html:
Voice & Delivery

Clear, steady pace throughout the talk.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #1a2028; border-radius: 12px; padding: 20px; } .ds-card-title { font-family: Geist, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.75rem; color: #e8eef4; margin: 0 0 12px 0; } .ds-card-body { font-family: Geist, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.5; color: #e8eef4; margin: 0; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Topic, audience context, and other form fields on the landing screen.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #1a2028; color: #e8eef4; border: 1px solid #2a323c; border-radius: 8px; padding: 10px 12px; font-family: Geist, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; width: 100%; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #8a96a3; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #6cc4ff; }

components.name: Score Ring
components.description: Signature component — the hero overall score readout on the report screen.
components.css: .ds-score-ring { width: 160px; height: 160px; border-radius: 9999px; border: 8px solid #1f9d55; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-score-ring-value { font-family: 'Geist Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-weight: 700; font-size: 4rem; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #1f9d55; } .ds-score-ring-suffix { font-family: Geist, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #8a96a3; }

components.name: Toast
components.description: Live-feedback warning during a rehearsal session.
components.html: Speaking a little fast on slide 3.
components.css: .ds-toast { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; background: #1a2028; border: 1px solid #2a323c; border-radius: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; min-width: 240px; font-family: Geist, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #e8eef4; animation: ds-toast-in 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } @keyframes ds-toast-in { from { opacity: 0; transform: translateX(40px); } to { opacity: 1; transform: translateX(0); } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-toast { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview: OctoPrep2000 borrows its visual logic from the performance-tracker category (Whoop, Oura): a single dark instrument panel where a number you can trust matters more than decoration. The system is precise, calm, and quiet by design — it never shouts a grade and never punishes a low score with alarm-red panels. A number on a dark field, supporting data in mono type, status communicated by both color and label so nothing depends on color alone. This system explicitly rejects the generic corporate-SaaS-dashboard look: no purple-to-blue gradients, no gray text dropped onto colored chips, no cards nested inside cards, no bounce or elastic motion, and no system-default typography.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black slate canvas, not pure black — feels like an instrument at rest, not a void.
One accent color (glacier sky blue), used sparingly, never as a page background.
A second typographic voice (mono) reserved for anything measured: scores, timestamps, session IDs.
Flat by default; elevation is tonal (a lighter slate surface), not shadow-driven.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Glacier Sky is the only saturated color with no semantic status meaning. If a new element needs emphasis and it isn't interactive, reach for typography weight or the Raised Slate elevation step before reaching for color.

rules.name: The Status-Plus-Label Rule
rules.body: Every status color (green/amber/red) ships with an icon or text label in the same component. No color-only signal, anywhere.

rules.name: The Two-Voice Rule
rules.body: Prose is Geist; anything that is a measurement (a score, a timestamp, a slide number, a session ID) is Geist Mono.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: No surface has a shadow in its default state. Shadows exist solely as hover/focus feedback on interactive elements.

dos: Do keep the dark Slate Night → Raised Slate tonal step as the only elevation mechanism.
Do render every measured value in Geist Mono.
Do pair every status color with an icon or text label, with no exceptions.
Do use ease-out-quart/expo for all transitions, with a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.
Do treat a low score as information: Signal Red appears on numbers/icons only, never as a panel or page background.
donts: Don't use Inter, Arial, or the literal system-ui font stack anywhere in this product.
Don't use a purple-to-blue gradient, or any gradient text.
Don't place gray (Slate Fog) text on any of the three status colors.
Don't nest a card/panel inside another card/panel.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing on any transition or entrance.
Don't use a colored side-stripe border on toasts, alerts, or list items.
Don't make the product feel like a generic corporate SaaS dashboard, a cartoon/gamified mascot experience, or a punitive grading screen.
